round_note: Refire on 2026-04-27 against the live tree. This witness supersedes the earlier same-path artifact and normalizes the gate counts.

alpha.consumed_as: background prior only; not load-bearing on Tier-1 promotion

beta.consumed_as: tie-break seed; no tie needed in this round

gamma.payload_summary: bridge_pick=bridge_simbad invocation=tool/simbad_bridge.hexa (real fetch deferred to sub-agent or OFFLINE-FALLBACK)
gamma.consumed_as: external bridge not invoked; offline fallback honored

core_5op_kernel: 3) fan out K>=3 orthogonal axes (raw 48 correlation < 0.7)
5) emit witness JSON to declared landing path

intake.package_description: Unified LLM API with automatic model discovery and provider configuration (originally forked from pi-mono)

compile_time_magic_sites: StreamFunction<TApi extends Api = Api, TOptions extends StreamOptions = StreamOptions>

intake.question: Given the live source, what port action has positive ROI now, and what strategy survives the falsifiers?
intake.interpretation: The file is the public contract surface for the hive-ai package: provider/API unions, model descriptors, message and event protocol, routing knobs, compat shims, and tool schemas. A useful answer must distinguish literal translation from generated bindings, package extraction, and boundary-only splits.

falsifiers_preregistered.name: size-bound-floor
falsifiers_preregistered.kills: ROI argument (surface is too trivial to justify a port analysis)

falsifiers_preregistered.name: consumer-count-floor
falsifiers_preregistered.evaluation: repo scan reports 106 unique .ts/.tsx importers of @need-singularity/hive-ai

falsifiers_preregistered.name: churn-rate-veto
falsifiers_preregistered.predicate: types.ts churn > 50% over the last 90 days
falsifiers_preregistered.evaluation: git history not consulted in this round

falsifiers_preregistered.name: binding-leakage-veto
falsifiers_preregistered.predicate: surface depends materially on TS-only or runtime-class bindings
falsifiers_preregistered.evaluation: Three concrete leakages are present: TS brand idiom on Api, TSchema-bound Tool.parameters, and runtime-class EventStream plus Model.compat conditional typing

falsifiers_preregistered.name: no-target-runtime
falsifiers_preregistered.predicate: topic names no concrete target language, runtime, or package
falsifiers_preregistered.kills: meaningful ROI number for a generic port ask
falsifiers_preregistered.evaluation: topic is 'ai types.ts port ROI' with no target declared

falsifiers_preregistered.name: schema-density-floor
falsifiers_preregistered.evaluation: Approximately 31 of 34 exports are pure data or data unions; ratio is about 0.91

falsifiers_preregistered.name: provider-vocabulary-rot-veto
falsifiers_preregistered.predicate: KnownApi / KnownProvider roster is a moving target
falsifiers_preregistered.evaluation: KnownApi has 10 entries, KnownProvider has 24 entries, and the package is already at version 0.71.0

falsifiers_preregistered.name: axis-orthogonality-floor
falsifiers_preregistered.predicate: K < 3 or max pairwise axis correlation >= 0.7
falsifiers_preregistered.evaluation: K=3 with max estimated pairwise correlation 0.55

options_enumerated.name: literal-port
options_enumerated.description: translate types.ts 1:1 into the target runtime
options_enumerated.verdict_after_falsifiers: Rejected as the default strategy. F4 and F7 jointly falsify it: there is a small but real host-bound region and the provider roster will drift.

options_enumerated.name: shim-adapter
options_enumerated.description: thin re-export or path-remap package only
options_enumerated.verdict_after_falsifiers: Viable only if the ask is packaging, not cross-runtime portability. Zero new capability otherwise.

options_enumerated.name: codegen-from-schema
options_enumerated.description: emit JSON-Schema or equivalent IR for the portable subset and generate per-target bindings
options_enumerated.verdict_after_falsifiers: Dominant when a target is named. F6 clears the density floor and F7 makes regeneration mandatory anyway.

options_enumerated.name: boundary-rewrite
options_enumerated.description: split portable IR from host-bound surface, then port only the IR
options_enumerated.verdict_after_falsifiers: Strong companion to DS-C because the host-bound surface is small and well-localized.

options_enumerated.name: extract-to-separate-package
options_enumerated.description: carve the contract into a lightweight @need-singularity/hive-ai-types package
options_enumerated.verdict_after_falsifiers: Useful precondition for DS-C or DS-D because 106 import sites do not need to pull the full root package shape forever.

gaps_identified: No maintainer-bandwidth falsifier for who would own the generator
No observed prior real port attempt in this repo family
No release-cadence measurement beyond the version and roster evidence

axes_fanout.implication: This file is materially more portable than the earlier extensions/types.ts case: smaller, denser in pure data, and cleaner at the host boundary. The portable core is large enough to justify generation once a target exists.

tier1_promotions.claim: Literal port is partially falsified, but only over a small region of the file. The practical blocker is not feasibility alone; it is the combination of localized TS/runtime leakage and inevitable roster drift.
ROI remains undefined as posed because no target runtime was declared. The correct first move is to restate the question as 'port ai/types.ts to X'.
Once a target is named, codegen-from-schema is the dominant strategy. The portable subset is large, regeneration is already required, and the residual hand-shim surface is bounded.
Extracting the contract into a dedicated lightweight package is a cheap precondition that improves downstream portability without forcing a full runtime port today.

tier2_held.claim: JSON-Schema should be the first generated artifact, followed by target-language bindings from the same IR.
tier2_held.needs: A named non-TS consumer and maintenance owner

tier2_held.claim: AssistantMessageEventStream is the only runtime-class binding that clearly deserves a hand-written bridge spec.
tier2_held.needs: Explicit stream protocol contract outside the TS class

tier2_held.claim: Any future port should be CI-gated to detect provider-roster drift automatically.
tier2_held.needs: Release cadence and generator hook design

deferred_or_unknown: F3 churn-rate-veto was left unknown rather than inferred

summary.headline: The ask still needs a target, but ai/types.ts is a strong future port candidate: about 91% of the export surface is portable data shape, while the non-portable residue is small and explicit.
summary.next_action: Re-pose as 'port ai/types.ts to {go|rust|python-pydantic|json-schema-only|separate-hive-package|hexa bridge}' and price DS-C plus DS-D for that target.
